reasoning:
Wei Yi's rating on 2025-10-30 was 2754. A 1% increase requires him to gain roughly 28 Elo points, bringing his rating to about 2782. At the super-GM level (2750+), gaining 28 points is extremely difficult and rare, as it requires dominant tournament performances against other top-level players (scoring +5 or +6 in classic super-tournaments). 

For the first resolution date (2025-11-16), this is just one week after the 2025-11-09 baseline. Since FIDE ratings are published monthly on the 1st of the month, the Wikipedia 'FIDE rankings' page will almost certainly show the exact same rating list on Nov 16 as it did on Nov 9. Therefore, exactly a 0% change is expected. 

For the second date (2025-12-09), this encompasses only one new FIDE rating list (December 2025). The probability of him gaining 28 points in a single month is extremely low. 

For the third date (2026-02-07), this gives three months of potential tournament play. While slightly more feasible than a one-month timeframe, reaching 2782 would still mean ascending to the very top echelon of chess (Top 5 globally). Given the empirical base rate of 0.007 for +1% Elo increases in similar questions, this remains highly improbable. 

Following the constraints to restrict probabilities to the 0.05 - 0.95 range and to increase uncertainty over time towards 0.5 for more distant horizons, I estimate the probabilities at 0.05, 0.06, and 0.07 respectively.
